 `./packages/rich-editor-demo/vite.config.ts`

```text
     1→import { existsSync } from 'node:fs'
     2→import { resolve } from 'node:path'
     3→
     4→import { vanillaExtractPlugin } from '@vanilla-extract/vite-plugin'
     5→import react from '@vitejs/plugin-react'
     6→import { codeInspectorPlugin } from 'code-inspector-plugin'
     7→import type { Plugin } from 'vite'
     8→import { defineConfig } from 'vite'
     9→
    10→// Dev-only: resolve workspace style entry to source instead of dist output.
    11→// - Prefer src/style.css for plain CSS packages.
    12→// - Fall back to src/styles.css.ts for VE packages when imported from JS.
    13→// - If imported from CSS (@import), VE entries resolve to empty CSS.
    14→function workspaceCssPlugin(): Plugin {
    15→  const scopes = new Set(['@haklex', '@shiro'])
    16→  const EMPTY_PREFIX = '\0empty-css:'
    17→  const VE_PREFIX = '\0ve-css:'
    18→
    19→  return {
    20→    name: 'workspace-css-dev',
    21→    apply: 'serve',
    22→    enforce: 'pre',
    23→    resolveId(id, importer) {
    24→      const m = id.match(/^@([\w-]+)\/([\w-]+)\/style\.css$/)
    25→      if (!m) return null
    26→
    27→      const scope = `@${m[1]}`
    28→      if (!scopes.has(scope)) return null
    29→
    30→      const pkgDir = m[2]
    31→      const srcIndex = resolve(__dirname, `../${pkgDir}/src/index.ts`)
    32→      const srcCss = resolve(__dirname, `../${pkgDir}/src/style.css`)
    33→      const srcVeCss = resolve(__dirname, `../${pkgDir}/src/styles.css.ts`)
    34→
    35→      if (existsSync(srcIndex)) {
    36→        if (importer?.endsWith('.css')) {
    37→          // Keep CSS graph valid; JS graph will import source modules and emit VE CSS.
    38→          return existsSync(srcCss) ? srcCss : `${EMPTY_PREFIX}${id}`
    39→        }
    40→        return `${VE_PREFIX}${srcIndex}`
    41→      }
    42→
    43→      if (existsSync(srcCss)) {
    44→        return srcCss
    45→      }
    46→
    47→      if (!existsSync(srcVeCss)) {
    48→        return null
    49→      }
    50→
    51→      // For CSS @import, VE source cannot be parsed as CSS.
    52→      if (importer?.endsWith('.css')) {
    53→        return `${EMPTY_PREFIX}${id}`
    54→      }
    55→
    56→      // For JS imports, route style.css to VE source for real-time compilation.
    57→      return `${VE_PREFIX}${srcVeCss}`
    58→    },
    59→    load(id) {
    60→      if (id.startsWith(EMPTY_PREFIX)) return ''
    61→      if (id.startsWith(VE_PREFIX)) {
    62→        const sourcePath = id.slice(VE_PREFIX.length)
    63→        return `import ${JSON.stringify(sourcePath)}`
    64→      }
    65→    },
    66→  }
    67→}
    68→
    69→export default defineConfig({
    70→  plugins: [
    71→    workspaceCssPlugin(),
    72→    vanillaExtractPlugin(),
    73→    codeInspectorPlugin({ bundler: 'vite', hotKeys: ['altKey'] }),
    74→    react(),
    75→  ],
    76→  server: {
    77→    port: 5188,
    78→    open: true,
    79→  },
    80→  optimizeDeps: {
    81→    include: ['react-intersection-observer', 'react-photo-view'],
    82→  },
    83→  resolve: {
    84→    dedupe: [
    85→      'lexical',
    86→      '@lexical/code',
    87→      '@lexical/link',
    88→      '@lexical/list',
    89→      '@lexical/markdown',
    90→      '@lexical/react',
    91→      '@lexical/rich-text',
    92→      '@lexical/table',
    93→      'react',
    94→      'react-dom',
    95→      'react/jsx-runtime',
    96→
    97→      'react-intersection-observer',
    98→      'react-photo-view',
    99→    ],
   100→  },
   101→})
   102→
```

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
The issue is that Vite's dev server doesn't watch files outside of its root directory by default. The workspace dependencies (like `@haklex/rich-editor`, `@haklex/rich-renderers`, etc.) are linked via pnpm workspace, but Vite doesn't know to watch those directories for changes.

I need to check how the workspace packages are structured - specifically whether the demo imports from `src` (source) or from the built `dist` output.

Let me check a few things:
1. How the workspace packages export their files (do they use `exports` pointing to `src` or `dist`?)


2. Whether Vite's `server.watch` configuration can be adjusted to follow symlinks and watch the workspace packages, since by default Vite ignores `node_modules` and won't pick up changes in symlinked packages.

I should examine the workspace package configurations to understand their export structure and then determine whether to configure Vite to watch the symlinked directories or adjust how the packages are being imported.
</details></think>

<tool-use data-tool-type="read" data-tool-name="Read"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `./packages/rich-editor-demo/vite.config.ts`

```text
     1→import { existsSync } from 'node:fs'
     2→import { resolve } from 'node:path'
     3→
     4→import { vanillaExtractPlugin } from '@vanilla-extract/vite-plugin'
     5→import react from '@vitejs/plugin-react'
     6→import { codeInspectorPlugin } from 'code-inspector-plugin'
     7→import type { Plugin } from 'vite'
     8→import { defineConfig } from 'vite'
     9→
    10→// Dev-only: resolve workspace style entry to source instead of dist output.
    11→// - Prefer src/style.css for plain CSS packages.
    12→// - Fall back to src/styles.css.ts for VE packages when imported from JS.
    13→// - If imported from CSS (@import), VE entries resolve to empty CSS.
    14→function workspaceCssPlugin(): Plugin {
    15→  const scopes = new Set(['@haklex', '@shiro'])
    16→  const EMPTY_PREFIX = '\0empty-css:'
    17→  const VE_PREFIX = '\0ve-css:'
    18→
    19→  return {
    20→    name: 'workspace-css-dev',
    21→    apply: 'serve',
    22→    enforce: 'pre',
    23→    resolveId(id, importer) {
    24→      const m = id.match(/^@([\w-]+)\/([\w-]+)\/style\.css$/)
    25→      if (!m) return null
    26→
    27→      const scope = `@${m[1]}`
    28→      if (!scopes.has(scope)) return null
    29→
    30→      const pkgDir = m[2]
    31→      const srcIndex = resolve(__dirname, `../${pkgDir}/src/index.ts`)
    32→      const srcCss = resolve(__dirname, `../${pkgDir}/src/style.css`)
    33→      const srcVeCss = resolve(__dirname, `../${pkgDir}/src/styles.css.ts`)
    34→
    35→      if (existsSync(srcIndex)) {
    36→        if (importer?.endsWith('.css')) {
    37→          // Keep CSS graph valid; JS graph will import source modules and emit VE CSS.
    38→          return existsSync(srcCss) ? srcCss : `${EMPTY_PREFIX}${id}`
    39→        }
    40→        return `${VE_PREFIX}${srcIndex}`
    41→      }
    42→
    43→      if (existsSync(srcCss)) {
    44→        return srcCss
    45→      }
    46→
    47→      if (!existsSync(srcVeCss)) {
    48→        return null
    49→      }
    50→
    51→      // For CSS @import, VE source cannot be parsed as CSS.
    52→      if (importer?.endsWith('.css')) {
    53→        return `${EMPTY_PREFIX}${id}`
    54→      }
    55→
    56→      // For JS imports, route style.css to VE source for real-time compilation.
    57→      return `${VE_PREFIX}${srcVeCss}`
    58→    },
    59→    load(id) {
    60→      if (id.startsWith(EMPTY_PREFIX)) return ''
    61→      if (id.startsWith(VE_PREFIX)) {
    62→        const sourcePath = id.slice(VE_PREFIX.length)
    63→        return `import ${JSON.stringify(sourcePath)}`
    64→      }
    65→    },
    66→  }
    67→}
    68→
    69→export default defineConfig({
    70→  plugins: [
    71→    workspaceCssPlugin(),
    72→    vanillaExtractPlugin(),
    73→    codeInspectorPlugin({ bundler: 'vite', hotKeys: ['altKey'] }),
    74→    react(),
    75→  ],
    76→  server: {
    77→    port: 5188,
    78→    open: true,
    79→  },
    80→  optimizeDeps: {
    81→    include: ['react-intersection-observer', 'react-photo-view'],
    82→  },
    83→  resolve: {
    84→    dedupe: [
    85→      'lexical',
    86→      '@lexical/code',
    87→      '@lexical/link',
    88→      '@lexical/list',
    89→      '@lexical/markdown',
    90→      '@lexical/react',
    91→      '@lexical/rich-text',
    92→      '@lexical/table',
    93→      'react',
    94→      'react-dom',
    95→      'react/jsx-runtime',
    96→
    97→      'react-intersection-observer',
    98→      'react-photo-view',
    99→    ],
   100→  },
   101→})
   102→
```
</details></tool-use>

So the file SHOULD be in Vite's module graph. And with our watcher plugin, chokidar should detect changes. 

But the user says it doesn't work. I think the problem might be with path normalization—Vite converts backslashes to forward slashes and resolves symlinks. More importantly, when `configureServer` runs, the module graph could still be empty since no modules have loaded yet. So when chokidar detects a file change and fires the event, Vite's HMR handler might not have the module in its graph to invalidate.

The real issue is probably whether `moduleGraph.getModulesByFile()` can actually find the modules when a file changes, since the graph stores modules by their normalized URLs and file paths in a `fileToModulesMap`.

When Vite loads a module, it sets the file path through the resolver's result, which uses `fs.realpathSync()` to resolve symlinks. So for `CodeBlockEditRenderer.tsx`, the stored path should be the fully resolved real path. When chokidar detects changes with `followSymlinks: true`, it reports the same real path, so they should match—unless there's a path normalization issue like case sensitivity differences, though that's unlikely on macOS APFS.

Let me try a different approach instead of debugging the default HMR: I'll add a custom watcher that listens directly to file changes in workspace packages and manually triggers HMR or a full reload.

But the user says nothing is happening, which suggests the module might not be in the graph at all, even though it should be. Maybe the watcher isn't detecting the change in the first place, so I should verify that chokidar is actually picking up the file modifications.

I can't test this directly, but the safest approach is to create a dedicated watcher for workspace packages and feed those changes into Vite's hot module system. Though I'm wondering if this is a Vite 7 API issue—maybe `server.ws` has changed or `server.watcher.add()` behaves differently now. Writing the plugin implementation... Writing the watcher logic...

I'm wondering if proper HMR is even possible here since Vite's core watcher might process the module change first, and I'm not sure if there's a race condition or if my handler would even be able to trigger the right HMR behavior after that.

<tool-use data-tool-type="read" data-tool-name="Read"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `./packages/rich-editor-demo/vite.config.ts`

```text
     1→import { existsSync, readdirSync } from 'node:fs'
     2→import { resolve } from 'node:path'
     3→
     4→import { vanillaExtractPlugin } from '@vanilla-extract/vite-plugin'
     5→import react from '@vitejs/plugin-react'
     6→import { codeInspectorPlugin } from 'code-inspector-plugin'
     7→import type { Plugin } from 'vite'
     8→import { defineConfig } from 'vite'
     9→
    10→// Dev-only: resolve workspace style entry to source instead of dist output.
    11→// - Prefer src/style.css for plain CSS packages.
    12→// - Fall back to src/styles.css.ts for VE packages when imported from JS.
    13→// - If imported from CSS (@import), VE entries resolve to empty CSS.
    14→function watchWorkspacePlugin(): Plugin {
    15→  return {
    16→    name: 'watch-workspace-packages',
    17→    apply: 'serve',
    18→    configureServer(server) {
    19→      const packagesDir = resolve(__dirname, '..')
    20→      for (const entry of readdirSync(packagesDir)) {
    21→        if (entry.startsWith('rich-') && entry !== 'rich-editor-demo') {
    22→          const srcDir = resolve(packagesDir, entry, 'src')
    23→          if (existsSync(srcDir)) {
    24→            server.watcher.add(srcDir)
    25→          }
    26→        }
    27→      }
    28→    },
    29→  }
    30→}
    31→
    32→function workspaceCssPlugin(): Plugin {
    33→  const scopes = new Set(['@haklex', '@shiro'])
    34→  const EMPTY_PREFIX = '\0empty-css:'
    35→  const VE_PREFIX = '\0ve-css:'
    36→
    37→  return {
    38→    name: 'workspace-css-dev',
    39→    apply: 'serve',
    40→    enforce: 'pre',
    41→    resolveId(id, importer) {
    42→      const m = id.match(/^@([\w-]+)\/([\w-]+)\/style\.css$/)
    43→      if (!m) return null
    44→
    45→      const scope = `@${m[1]}`
    46→      if (!scopes.has(scope)) return null
    47→
    48→      const pkgDir = m[2]
    49→      const srcIndex = resolve(__dirname, `../${pkgDir}/src/index.ts`)
    50→      const srcCss = resolve(__dirname, `../${pkgDir}/src/style.css`)
    51→      const srcVeCss = resolve(__dirname, `../${pkgDir}/src/styles.css.ts`)
    52→
    53→      if (existsSync(srcIndex)) {
    54→        if (importer?.endsWith('.css')) {
    55→          // Keep CSS graph valid; JS graph will import source modules and emit VE CSS.
    56→          return existsSync(srcCss) ? srcCss : `${EMPTY_PREFIX}${id}`
    57→        }
    58→        return `${VE_PREFIX}${srcIndex}`
    59→      }
    60→
    61→      if (existsSync(srcCss)) {
    62→        return srcCss
    63→      }
    64→
    65→      if (!existsSync(srcVeCss)) {
    66→        return null
    67→      }
    68→
    69→      // For CSS @import, VE source cannot be parsed as CSS.
    70→      if (importer?.endsWith('.css')) {
    71→        return `${EMPTY_PREFIX}${id}`
    72→      }
    73→
    74→      // For JS imports, route style.css to VE source for real-time compilation.
    75→      return `${VE_PREFIX}${srcVeCss}`
    76→    },
    77→    load(id) {
    78→      if (id.startsWith(EMPTY_PREFIX)) return ''
    79→      if (id.startsWith(VE_PREFIX)) {
    80→        const sourcePath = id.slice(VE_PREFIX.length)
    81→        return `import ${JSON.stringify(sourcePath)}`
    82→      }
    83→    },
    84→  }
    85→}
    86→
    87→export default defineConfig({
    88→  plugins: [
    89→    watchWorkspacePlugin(),
    90→    workspaceCssPlugin(),
    91→    vanillaExtractPlugin(),
    92→    codeInspectorPlugin({ bundler: 'vite', hotKeys: ['altKey'] }),
    93→    react(),
    94→  ],
    95→  server: {
    96→    port: 5188,
    97→    open: true,
    98→  },
    99→  optimizeDeps: {
   100→    include: ['react-intersection-observer', 'react-photo-view'],
   101→  },
   102→  resolve: {
   103→    dedupe: [
   104→      'lexical',
   105→      '@lexical/code',
   106→      '@lexical/link',
   107→      '@lexical/list',
   108→      '@lexical/markdown',
   109→      '@lexical/react',
   110→      '@lexical/rich-text',
   111→      '@lexical/table',
   112→      'react',
   113→      'react-dom',
   114→      'react/jsx-runtime',
   115→
   116→      'react-intersection-observer',
   117→      'react-photo-view',
   118→    ],
   119→  },
   120→})
   121→
```
